events.title: Rock the Block
events.description: The fun is all about out on the block this summer. Get outdoors and join us on the back patio and field as we bring out the music, dance, play some games, have a talent show, stay cool by the pool, and enjoy summertime in the city. It’s an active, fun adventure everyday and the staff at the park will make sure that it Rocks the Block! *No Program June 19th or July 4th.

events.title: Meadow Makers
events.description:
Wednesdays, 9:30–11 am, May 20 – September 2, 2026

Join in the meadow-making in Theodore Wirth Regional Park!

Stop by the meadow off the entrance drive to the Eloise Butler Wildflower Garden and join in the weeding and planting fun.

No registration required. Stay for as little or as long as you’d like!

events.title: Garden Storytime
events.description:
Thursdays, May 7-October 8, 10-10:30 am

Sundays, May 17-October 11, 10 am – 10:30am

Come for a nature-packed Storytime in the heart of the Wildflower Garden led by a Naturalist. Read stories and sing songs before exploring the trails or making a themed craft in the Martha Crone Visitor Shelter. Perfect for ages 0-7 with their caregivers present.

No registration necessary, meet outside the Martha Crone Visitor Shelter.

events.title: Early Birders – September
events.description:
Saturdays in September: 8:30-10:30am

Learn to observe and identify birds by sound and sight as you walk through the Garden and Wirth Park with a Garden Naturalist.

No registration necessary, meet at the Garden’s parking lot.

events.title: Making Space for Native Plants in Greater Eloise Stewardship Area
events.description:
Join the Greater Eloise Butler Stewards to remove invasive plants!

Dates: Second and fourth Sundays, 2-4:30pm

Location: Eloise Butler. Follow our signs from the Garden’s front gate kiosk to the weeding site.
We work adjacent to Eloise Butler Wildflower Garden in Theodore Wirth Park. (The Garden itself is maintained by dedicated Minneapolis Park Board staff. Our work helps protect their efforts.)

Supplies: We provide tools, bags, training and gloves. Bring your own water and snacks.

NOTE: There may be ticks and poison ivy in the area. Dress appropriately (long sleeves, pants, and closed-toe shoes).

No need to register ahead of time. No experience necessary.

Learn more here. link to https://www.friendsofeloisebutler.org/
